original_hypothesis.title: Permanent Fund Lockup via Complete Owner Removal
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: `MultiOwnable` does not enforce a minimum owner count. A user (or a compromised owner) can call `removeOwnerAtIndex` to remove all registered owners, including themselves. Once the last owner is removed, the wallet is permanently bricked. No further transactions can be executed, no new owners can be added, and the implementation cannot be upgraded.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The lack of a minimum threshold check allows the state machine to enter a dead-end state (zero owners) from which recovery is mathematically impossible, leading to permanent loss of funds.

proof_of_signal.pattern: removeOwnerAtIndex deletes the owner without checking if remaining active owners > 0

preconditions: User accidentally removes all owners, OR
Attacker compromises one owner and maliciously removes all owners to grief the user

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No lower bound check on active owner count during removal
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker submits a UserOp to remove the last remaining owner
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Pure griefing attack; attacker gains no funds but permanently destroys the victim's access to their assets

economic_questions: Can this be weaponized in a ransom scenario (e.g., 'pay me or I remove the last owner')?
Are there any fallback recovery mechanisms in the EntryPoint or Factory?
validation_requirements: Simulate removing all owners and attempt to execute a transaction
Verify that no other code path can add an owner when the count is zero

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Anytime after an owner key is compromised

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Users who make a mistake or have a single key compromised
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Total loss of access to funds

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
